criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Local providers, clinics, therapists, and care
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 94 public provider rows and 23 staged provider rows across all 50 states.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: This is the biggest information hole if the goal is all local help, especially to compete on care and treatment discovery.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Advocates and legal/IEP support
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 2995 advocate rows, with California still blocked in strict gold because 58 counties lose advocate coverage after truth gating.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: Advocate count is decent, but truth-safe local coverage is not yet strong enough to call exhaustive.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Findhelp-like metadata: availability, accessibility, intake, capacity
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: Provider accessibility booleans with true signal: accepts_medi_cal 27/94; interpreter_available 22/94; asl_available 12/94; wheelchair_accessible 5/94; virtual_services 18/94; in_person_services 60/94; home_visits 3/94; transportation_help 8/94 Nonprofit accessibility booleans with true signal: interpreter_available 0/29501; asl_available 0/29501; wheelchair_accessible 0/29501; virtual_services 1/29501; in_person_services 22/29501; home_visits 0/29501; transportation_help 0/29501 Advocate accessibility booleans with true signal: interpreter_available 0/2995; asl_available 0/2995; wheelchair_accessible 0/2995; virtual_services 2/2995; in_person_services 0/2995; home_visits 0/2995; transportation_help 0/2995
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: The schema exists, but live high-signal metadata is sparse, especially on nonprofits and advocates.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Canonical org -> program -> location normalization
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 36891 organizations, 36891 org-program links, 83 service locations, 4314 office locations, 33130 virtual service areas.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: Normalization exists, but service-location depth is still too thin to support a fully location-rich help product.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Help content and explanatory knowledge
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: The content layer is still far too small for the full family journey nationally, but staged knowledge growth is now present and measured.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Family workflow, case tracking, reminders, and documents
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 5 total family-case-layer rows across 8 tables; most tables are empty.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: The runtime workflow model exists but is not populated as a real product layer.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: Support planning, collaboration, and care coordination
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: This entire product surface is still schema-only in practice.

criticalExhaustiveGaps.label: User submissions, coverage gaps, verification queue
criticalExhaustiveGaps.evidence: 0 user submissions, 0 coverage gaps, 2 verification queue items.
criticalExhaustiveGaps.exhaustiveGap: Operational feedback loops are not built out enough to sustain exhaustive maintenance.
